prerequisites: A Jina AI API key is optional. Without one, requests use the anonymous pool, which is the most aggressively rate-limited; providing a key raises your rate limit.
To obtain a key, generate one from the Jina AI API dashboard (jina.ai/api-dashboard). New keys come with free tokens.

Decide which optional behaviors you need so you can set the corresponding config fields: gather_images and gather_links to append image and link summaries, and read_prompt and search_prompt to customize Reader and Search requests.

authMethods.name: API key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: Maps to the connector's optional api_key secret field. The Jina AI Reader API authenticates via an Authorization: Bearer <api_key> header. The key is optional: anonymous requests work but are the most aggressively rate-limited, while an authenticated key raises your quota. Generate a key from the Jina AI API dashboard.

setupSteps.title: Get a Jina AI API key (optional)
setupSteps.body: Go to the Jina AI API dashboard at jina.ai/api-dashboard and generate an API key. New keys come with free tokens. You can skip this step and run anonymously, but anonymous traffic is the most aggressively rate-limited.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key
setupSteps.body: Enter the key in the connector's api_key field. It is sent as an Authorization: Bearer <api_key> header to the Jina Reader endpoints (r.jina.ai for reading URLs and s.jina.ai for search). Leave api_key empty to use anonymous, rate-limited access.

setupSteps.title: Configure optional extraction and prompt fields
setupSteps.body: Set gather_images and gather_links to control whether the connector appends summaries of images and links from fetched pages. Optionally set read_prompt and search_prompt to customize the Reader (read) and Search behavior for your use case.
